channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Before Apple releases a television, it needs to fix its TV app, and more
item.description: Earlier this month, the tech sphere was buzzing following a report that Apple was “evaluating” the idea of releasing a television.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Popular free alternative to Adobe Illustrator gets major upgrade
item.description: Inkscape 1.4 offers new features like filter gallery and modular grids for improved design flexibility and productivity.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Home entertainment holiday gift ideas at a discount
item.description: The industry buzz is we will see repeats between now and Christmas so if you miss the deal first time around, keep checking.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Robots perform like human surgeons by just watching videos
item.description: University scientists have trained a robotic surgical system to do work with the skill of human doctors, possibly transforming the future of surgeries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Canadian news outlets sue OpenAI over copyright infringement
item.description: Five leading Canadian news outlets filed a lawsuit against OpenAI on Friday, accusing the ChatGPT owner of violating copyright laws to train its artificial intelligence (AI) models.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two years of ChatGPT: the conversation that never ends
item.description: Two years can be an eternity for the technology industry: plenty of time for enterprises to innovate, launch a new product, peak on the stock market… and then plummet again. Think about the bubbles that briefly surrounded 3D printing, smart glasses, or the metaverse.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stretching comms to the limit in the wake of a natural disaster
item.description: The effects of a brutal storm that hit the eastern Spanish peninsula in late October were felt beyond the affected areas. Videos shared on social media and calls to television and radio programs showed the magnitude of the rain and flooding in Valencia, Albacete, and other towns in the area.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Certifications for Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) - Overview
item.description: Learn about various SAFe certifications and requirements for program managers, Scrum masters, team leads, and other roles.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Biden’s AI tech could play a role in Trump’s immigration crackdown
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration—among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to he
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Amazon's $4B bet on an AI startup
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 sneaky gift card scams to watch out for this holiday season
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explores six sneaky holiday season gift card tricks, providing tips on how to outsmart scammers trying to rip you off.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kotlin for Java developers: Concurrency with coroutines
item.description: The ability to perform multiple operations simultaneously is one of the most important aspects of any programming language. Orchestrating multiple paths of execution is inherently complex and there are various approaches to taming this complexity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A software developer gives thanks
item.description: Tomorrow, we in the US celebrate Thanksgiving. So today seemed like a good time for me to recognize the people and technologies in the software development world that I’m most thankful for.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australia’s lower House passes social media ban for under-16s
item.description: Communications Minister Michelle Rowland said the Senate would debate the bill later Wednesday. The major parties' support all but guarantees the legislation will pass in the Senate, where no party holds a majority of seats.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: This TikTok user says a popular YouTube music channel is in fact AI
item.description: Are you really a student if you’ve not done a late night study session soundtracked to an unending lo-fi beats playlist on YouTube? According to Google Trends, searches for the term “lo-fi” on YouTube have tripled since 2020.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: At the Pacific Northwest National Laboratory automated testing is part of the design
item.description: Paul Reichlin director of digital platforms at the Pacific Northwest National Laboratory sees automated software testing as a foundational role. The post At the Pacific Northwest National Laboratory automated testing is part of the design first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: AI PCs for federal: The future of edge computing
item.description: How AI PCs supplemented by extreme search capabilities will enable federal employees at the edge to leverage more data to make better decisions, faster. The post AI PCs for federal: The future of edge computing first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: 10 ways AI can make farmers’ jobs easier
item.description: For those who work the earth, AI can be an invaluable tool for making that work more efficient, sustainable and productive. AI helps farmers use the same tools they have been using for centuries, but with improved efficiency and focus.
item.dc:creator: Richard Baguley, Fast Company

item.title: Linux command line for beginners: 25 essential commands
item.description: Getting started on the Linux command line might seem overwhelming at first, but the many commands you need to use will fall into place more quickly than you might imagine. Let’s start with some introductory but very important commands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Influencer houses are dead. Welcome to the production studio era
item.description: Influencers today look a lot more like Hollywood executives than ragtag social media users. And they’re buying up the real estate to prove it. A slew of creators have been buying plots of studio land to film and edit their content.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Cisco, NTT Data partner to simplify private 5G connectivity
item.description: Cisco and NTT Data are extending their partnership to offer customers more streamlined options for deploying private 5G services for enterprise connectivity. The partnership ties Cisco’s networking technologies to NTT Data’s Transatel network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: OPM tries a shift-left approach to anticipating the costs of new tech projects
item.description: When thinking of new technology projects, OPM officials are trying to get a handler on potential costs. The post OPM tries a shift-left approach to anticipating the costs of new tech projects first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Lost access? Here’s how to reclaim your Facebook account
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how to regain your Facebook access if you get hacked or locked out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Interpol clamps down on cybercrime and arrests over 1,000 suspects in Africa
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Interpol arrested 1,006 suspects in Africa during a massive two-month operation, clamping down on cybercrime that left tens of thousands of victims, including some who were trafficked, and produced millions in financial damages, the global police organization said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Tesla excluded from EV buyer credits in Gov. Newsom’s proposal
item.description: Excluding Tesla could burnish Newsom’s standing on the left as he renews a clash with Musk, who has become a member of Trump’s inner circle and accepted a role helping the incoming administration cut government spending.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Build generative AI pipelines without the infrastructure headache
item.description: While creating a basic ChatGPT prototype might take a weekend, developing production-ready generative AI systems that securely handle enterprise data presents significantly greater engineering challenges.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Automaker spying on drivers, reporting data to insurance companies
item.description: Since April, seven class action lawsuits have been filed against General Motors, OnStar, and data brokers LexisNexis and Verisk Analytics. The suits accuse GM of partnering with data brokers to collect driving data without proper consent using OnStar Smart Driver technology.
item.dc:creator: Mike Colombo, NewsNation

item.title: Rethinking defense technology: A blueprint for US success in a complex global landscape
item.description: The Defense Department has made efforts to enhance its AI capabilities through the AI Adoption Strategy. The post Rethinking defense technology: A blueprint for US success in a complex global landscape first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: North Korean hackers use disguised apps to target Macs with hidden malware
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says North Korean hackers hide malware in macOS apps, bypassing Mac security checks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
